title: androsace occidentalis subsp. arizonica
intro: Androsace occidentalis subsp. arizonica is a rare, endemic species of flowering plant in the primulaceae family, native to Arizona, known as the Arizona Rock Jasmine. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Androsace occidentalis subsp. arizonica is typically used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
